intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1, P2, P3, and P4 are not identifiable as living specimens in the provided imagery. The pots appear to contain only soil and debris.

plant_audit.P3: Empty/Soil only. The white rabbit anchor is present but the Pothos is absent.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and undisturbed across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: The presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or mineral deposits) in the soil of the foreground pot is noted as an intervention/debris.

temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Total loss of visual data (black frames).
temporal_deltas.summary: The sequence shows a transition from visible, empty pots to complete visual obstruction (blackout).

vision_report.visual_health_inference: All registered plants are confirmed as 'Dead/Lost'. The biome is currently non-viable for the listed species.
vision_report.anomalies: The final three images (T-2, T-1, CURRENT) are completely black, indicating a failure in the visual monitoring system or a complete lack of light in the environment.
vision_report.narrative_description: My audit confirms a total systemic collapse of the botanical registry. The initial images show pots containing only soil and minor debris (eggshell fragments). There is no evidence of the String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, or Pothos. The white rabbit anchor is visible in the early frames but serves no purpose as the host plant is absent. The final frames are entirely obscured, preventing any further observation of the desk environment.
